decisions.what: WordReveal splits text by whitespace and matches highlight words against punctuation-stripped lowercase form
decisions.why: Keeps headline highlight matching robust if a target word appears with trailing punctuation, while preserving the original word for visual rendering

decisions.what: WordReveal renders a screen-reader-only span with the full text and aria-hidden on the animated per-word visual span
decisions.why: Per spec: assistive tech reads the full headline once instead of word-by-word fragments; matches accessibility-first animation pattern

decisions.what: Hero uses three motion.div orbs with staggered easeInOut scale/opacity loops, disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set
decisions.why: Spec calls for animated glow orbs; respecting reduced-motion preference matches the project's motion conventions (mirrors MorphingButton/useReducedMotion usage)

decisions.what: Hero secondary 'Learn More' is a plain styled button rather than a MorphingButton
decisions.why: Spec describes it as a 'Secondary button' that scrolls to #features without async state; MorphingButton's onClick contract is async/Promise-based, so a regular button matches the intended UX

decisions.what: Subheadline and CTA delays (1.5s, 2s) are zeroed when prefers-reduced-motion is true
decisions.why: Avoids long invisible delays for users with reduced motion preferences while preserving the staggered choreography for everyone else

decisions.what: App.tsx main no longer applies pt-16 md:pt-20
decisions.why: Per spec: Hero handles its own spacing via min-h-screen and the fixed header naturally overlays the hero
